command:
cd /testbed && python manage.py runserver &
curl -I http://localhost:8000/
sed -i '' '/class MyChoice(models.TextChoices):/a\    def __str__(self):\n        return self.value' testbed/models.py
sed -i '' '/class MyChoice(models.TextChoices):/a\    def __str__(self):\n        return self.value' tests/models.py
sed -i '' '/class MyChoice(models.TextChoices):/a\    def __str__(self):\n        return self.value' tests/models.py

sed -i '/def display_for_field(obj, field, model_admin=None):/a\    if isinstance(field, models.JSONField):\n        return field.prepare_value(getattr(obj, field.name))' django/contrib/admin/utils.py

echo "from django.contrib import admin\nfrom django.db import models" > test_script.py
echo "class MyModel(models.Model):\n    data = models.JSONField()" >> test_script.py
echo "@admin.register(MyModel)\nclass MyModelAdmin(admin.ModelAdmin):\n    readonly_fields = ('data',)" >> test_script.py
python manage.py runserver &
sleep 5
curl http://localhost:8000/admin/myapp/mymodel/
killall python

cd /testbed
django-admin startproject myproject .
cd myproject
django-admin startapp myapp
echo "from django.contrib import admin\nfrom django.db import models" > myapp/models.py
echo "class MyModel(models.Model):\n    data = models.JSONField()" >> myapp/models.py
echo "@admin.register(MyModel)\nclass MyModelAdmin(admin.ModelAdmin):\n    readonly_fields = ('data',)" >> myapp/admin.py
echo "INSTALLED_APPS += ['myapp']" >> settings.py
python manage.py makemigrations
python manage.py migrate
python manage.py runserver &
sleep 5
curl http://localhost:8000/admin/myapp/mymodel/
killall python

touch myproject/manage.py
echo "import os
import sys

def main():
    """Run administrative tasks."""
    os.environ.setdefault('DJANGO_SETTINGS_MODULE', 'myproject.settings')
    try:
        from django.core.management import execute_from_command_line
    except ImportError as exc:
        raise ImportError(
            "Couldn't import Django. Are you sure it's installed and 
            available on your PYTHONPATH environment variable? Did you 
            forget to activate a virtual environment?
        ) from exc
    execute_from_command_line(sys.argv)

if __name__ == '__main__':
    main()" > myproject/manage.py
cd myproject
django-admin startapp myapp
echo "from django.contrib import admin
from django.db import models" > myapp/models.py
echo "class MyModel(models.Model):
    data = models.JSONField()" >> myapp/models.py
echo "@admin.register(MyModel)
class MyModelAdmin(admin.ModelAdmin):
    readonly_fields = ('data',)" >> myapp/admin.py
echo "INSTALLED_APPS += ['myapp']" >> settings.py
python manage.py makemigrations
python manage.py migrate
python manage.py runserver &
sleep 5
curl http://localhost:8000/admin/myapp/mymodel/
killall python
